updates.note: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari announced that the state government has directed all relevant departments to immediately hand over land to railway authorities to facilitate the completion of 61 stalled railway projects, including the Hili and Nandigram projects. The administration also cleared long-pending police permissions to resume construction of the New Garia-Airport metro viaduct at Chingrighata crossing. State departments are currently coordinating the land transfer process to resolve these bottlenecks.

Union Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw informed Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari of the approval for multiple railway projects, including a new Kolkata-Jaipur train, a third line survey between Shalimar and Ayodhya, and doubling of the NJP-Siliguri corridor. These approvals follow recent state-level efforts to expedite long-pending railway infrastructure in West Bengal.
